title: glandularia aurantiaca var. glaberrima
intro: Glandularia aurantiaca var. glaberrima, also known as the Glaberrima or Orange Buttercup, is a flowering plant species within the Verbenaceae family, native to South America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The plant is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. Herbal preparations of Glandularia aurantiaca var. glaberrima can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
